topic: A person accidentally sends a text to the wrong recipient
raw_output:
[story 1]

My thumb hovered over the screen, a cold sweat prickling my palms. I’d intended to send a quick, innocuous meme to Sarah, but my eyes had drifted, landing instead on a contact labeled 'CEO - Mr. Sterling.' A jolt of icy dread shot through me. My breath hitched, a tiny, shallow sound in the quiet apartment. With a shaky finger, I typed out the silly picture, then, with a panicked, almost involuntary jerk, I hit send. The silence that followed was deafening. I stared at the phone, heart hammering against my ribs like a trapped bird, waiting for the inevitable explosion.

[story 2]

The fluorescent lights of the office hummed, magnifying every bead of perspiration on my forehead. I reread the message three times. It was meant for my colleague, Mark, a simple query about a shared project deadline. But my finger slipped, a clumsy motion, and the recipient field flickered, settling on the number for the department head. A wave of nausea washed over my stomach, making it hard to focus on anything else. My hands began to tremble visibly, and I could feel the blood draining from my face. I squeezed my eyes shut for a brief second, praying the universe would somehow rewind the last three seconds.

[story 3]

I leaned back in my chair, the worn leather creaking beneath me. The phone felt impossibly heavy in my hand. A ridiculous thought—sending a casual joke to my grandmother instead of my boss—had spiraled into a catastrophe. My gaze darted between the message composition window and the contact list, a frantic search for an escape route that didn't exist. My jaw tightened so hard I could feel the strain in my temples. When the notification finally popped up, a sharp intake of air escaped me, and I instinctively pressed my back against the cool wall, wishing I could simply dissolve into the wallpaper.
